items.a: AM is a digital worker — an autonomous AI that handles end-to-end job functions for PMs, developers, and support teams. For PMs, it triages backlogs, grooms sprints, and drafts release notes. For developers, it picks up tickets, writes code, and opens PRs. For support teams, it resolves tickets autonomously and escalates edge cases. AM doesn't just respond to prompts — it owns outcomes.

items.q: How is AM different from GitHub Copilot, Devin, or Intercom Fin?
items.a: Copilot is a coding assistant — it helps you write code, but you're still doing the work. Devin is end-to-end for engineering only. Fin is end-to-end for support only. AM covers all three team functions — PM, developer, and support — under one platform. It also has persistent context across your whole team, not just one user or one workflow.

AM integrates with Jira, Linear, GitHub, GitLab, Slack, Zendesk, Intercom, Notion, Confluence, and more. It works inside your existing tools — it doesn't require you to change your workflow. We add integrations based on what our customers use, and enterprise customers can request custom integrations.

Yes. AM processes your data under a data processing agreement and does not train on your company's data. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest. We support SSO, role-based access controls, and audit logs for enterprise customers. We're SOC 2 Type II in progress.

Onboarding takes 1–2 hours. We connect AM to your tools (GitHub, Jira, Slack, etc.), give it read access to your codebase and knowledge base, and run a context-building session. Within the first week, AM has enough context to handle real work. We provide a dedicated success engineer for teams on Growth and above.

items.q: What happens when AM doesn't know something or makes a mistake?
items.a: AM is built to escalate, not fail silently. When it encounters a situation outside its confidence threshold — a regulatory edge case, an ambiguous customer situation, a complex architectural decision — it surfaces the issue to a human with full context and a recommended path forward. We track escalation rates and work with you to improve them over time.

items.q: Can AM work autonomously overnight or on a schedule?
items.a: Yes. AM runs on schedules, triggers, and events. You can configure it to triage incoming support tickets continuously, run a nightly backlog review, or post a weekly sprint summary to Slack every Monday morning — without anyone asking. It works while your team is offline.

AM is free and open source. You can self-host it and use it indefinitely at no cost. We offer optional paid support services ($50 per 30-minute session) for training, custom integrations, and strategic consulting.

items.q: We're not ready to buy — can we try AM first?
items.a: Yes. AM is free forever. Download it from GitHub, self-host it, and start using it immediately. If you need guidance on setup, training, or custom integrations, we offer paid support sessions at $50 per 30 minutes.

items.q: Why does AM support Spanish and Chinese?
items.a: Removing language barriers accelerates global adoption. Our users span 40+ countries across Latin America, Spain, China, and Southeast Asia. By supporting Spanish and Chinese natively, we make AM accessible to teams regardless of their language. This reflects our belief that powerful tools should be available to everyone, globally, without translation friction.

hero.subhead: Pair AM with each person on your team. A 3-person team moves at the velocity of 20 — without the payroll, taxes, insurance, or meetings.

personas.headline: Stop managing process. Start managing product.
personas.pain: 60% of PM time goes to triage, grooming, status updates, and meeting prep — not product thinking.
personas.value: AM handles the backlog, grooms tickets before every sprint, drafts release notes, and writes the weekly stakeholder update. Your PMs show up to every meeting ready and spend their time on the work that actually requires them.

personas.headline: Ship the backlog. Kill the context switches.
personas.pain: Engineers spend 40% of their time on maintenance, migrations, documentation, and code review — not building.
personas.value: AM picks up tickets, reads the codebase, writes the code, and opens PRs. It reviews incoming PRs like a senior engineer, handles migrations, and answers codebase questions instantly. Your engineers build, not babysit.

personas.headline: Resolve more tickets. Hire fewer people.
personas.pain: Ticket volume grows faster than you can hire. CSAT drops. Nights and weekends go uncovered.
personas.value: AM resolves tickets autonomously 24/7, escalates edge cases with full context, and gets better with every interaction. Your team handles the complex cases that need human judgment — not the hundred tickets that don't.

features.headline: One worker, three team functions.
features.subhead: AM covers the work your PMs, developers, and support teams spend their time on — not just a task here and there.

items.title: Backlog Triage
items.body: AM reads every incoming ticket, labels it, prioritizes it against sprint goals, and surfaces what needs immediate attention. Your backlog stays clean without you touching it.

items.title: Sprint Grooming
items.body: AM prepares tickets for grooming — writes acceptance criteria, identifies missing context, flags blockers, and estimates complexity based on your team's history. Show up to grooming with a ready board.

items.title: Release Notes & Changelogs
items.body: AM reads merged PRs and writes release notes in your product voice. Customer-facing changelog, internal engineering summary, and stakeholder email — generated and ready to send.

items.title: Meeting Notes → Tickets
items.body: Drop in a meeting transcript. AM extracts action items, creates tickets with owners and due dates, and links them to the right epics. Every decision becomes a tracked item.

items.title: PRD Drafts
items.stat: From idea to doc in minutes
items.body: Tell AM what you're building. It drafts the PRD — problem statement, success metrics, user stories, open questions. You refine the thinking, not the formatting.

items.title: Stakeholder Updates
items.body: AM monitors sprint progress and auto-drafts weekly stakeholder updates. One click to send — no manual status aggregation, no chasing engineers for updates.

items.title: Ticket → Pull Request
items.body: Assign AM a Jira or Linear ticket. It reads the codebase, writes the code, opens a PR with tests, and posts a summary for review. End-to-end task ownership without the hand-holding.

items.title: Code Review Partner
items.body: AM reviews incoming PRs for correctness, test coverage, edge cases, and adherence to your team's patterns. It comments like a senior engineer — specific, actionable, not nitpicky.

items.title: On-Call Triage
items.body: AM monitors alerts, correlates incidents with recent deployments, and surfaces the most likely root cause before you're fully awake. Less time diagnosing, more time fixing.

items.title: Migration & Refactor Work
items.body: AM handles boilerplate-heavy migrations — library upgrades, linting rule enforcement, API version bumps across hundreds of files. Systematic work that blocks your roadmap but no one wants to do.

items.title: Documentation
items.body: AM reads your codebase and writes inline docs, README updates, and architecture decision records. Your next hire understands the system faster.

items.title: Codebase Q&A
items.body: Ask AM anything about your codebase. Where is the auth logic? What does this function do? Why was this approach chosen? It has read everything and answers like someone who wrote it.

items.title: Autonomous Ticket Resolution
items.body: AM resolves support tickets end-to-end — reads the issue, finds the answer in your knowledge base, and sends a response in your brand voice. Human escalation only when genuinely needed.

items.title: 24/7 Coverage
items.body: AM covers nights, weekends, and spikes without staffing changes. Your customers get responses in under two minutes regardless of when they write in.

items.title: Smart Escalation
items.body: AM knows when a ticket needs a human — regulatory issues, edge cases, frustrated customers. It escalates with full context: the conversation, the customer's history, and a recommended next step.

items.title: Knowledge Base Maintenance
items.body: AM identifies gaps in your knowledge base from recurring ticket themes and drafts new articles to fill them. Your docs get better the more AM works.

items.title: CSAT & Trend Analysis
items.body: AM tracks patterns in ticket volume, resolution time, and customer sentiment. It surfaces emerging issues before they become incidents — and tells you why CSAT moved last week.

items.title: Multilingual Support
items.body: AM reads and responds in over 40 languages. Respond to your French, Spanish, Japanese, or German customers in their language, with the same quality and voice.

items.title: Persistent Context
items.body: AM builds a model of your team — codebase, customer history, product decisions, team patterns. It brings this context to every task, every time, without needing to be caught up.

items.title: Outcome Ownership
items.body: AM owns end-to-end outcomes, not just individual responses. Give it a goal, not a task. It plans the steps, executes them, and reports back — like a team member, not a tool.

items.title: Works While You Sleep
items.body: AM runs on triggers, schedules, and events. Morning backlog summary, nightly build health report, weekly stakeholder digest — all autonomous, all delivered without anyone asking.

items.title: Ecosystem Agnostic
items.body: AM integrates with Jira, Linear, GitHub, Slack, Zendesk, Intercom, Notion, and more. It works in your existing workflow — not a new one you have to adopt.

soulRestore.subhead: AM runs locally in the background, constantly working on the tasks and goals you have assigned. Run multiple projects simultaneously — sales pipelines, customer support, dev, content. If you can visualize it on a board and the delivery is electronic, AM will become your new best friend.

pillars.label: Always running
pillars.body: AM works in the background 24/7 — no prompting, no babysitting. Assign a goal and she goes to work.

pillars.label: Multiple projects at once
pillars.body: Sales pipelines, customer support, dev, content — run them all simultaneously across separate boards.

pillars.label: Link your AMs
pillars.body: Small team, each running AM? Link them together. They negotiate trust, share context, and collaborate on high-level plans — so your whole team is augmented, not just one person.

soulRestore.closerHeadline1: AM isn’t a tool you use.
soulRestore.closerBody: She shows up every day, knows your context, and gets the work done — so you can focus on what actually needs you.
soulRestore.body: AM runs locally in the background, constantly working on the tasks and goals you have assigned. Run multiple projects simultaneously — sales pipelines, customer support, website and software development, content management. If you can visualize it on a board and the delivery is electronic, AM will become your new best friend.

points.label: Multiple projects at once
points.body: Sales pipelines, customer support, dev, content — run them all simultaneously across separate boards.

points.label: Link your AMs
points.body: Small team, each running AM? Link them together. They negotiate trust, share context, and collaborate on high-level plans — so your whole team is augmented, not just one person.

device.subhead: AM works like a senior team member — not a tool you prompt. You assign work. AM owns it. You review the result.

steps.title: Connect your tools
steps.body: AM integrates with your existing stack — Jira, Linear, GitHub, Slack, Zendesk, Intercom, Notion. Setup takes under two hours. AM reads your codebase, knowledge base, and historical data to build context.

steps.title: Assign work like a teammate
steps.body: Assign AM a ticket, a goal, or a whole workflow. It plans the steps, executes them end-to-end, and works on your schedule — including nights and weekends. No prompt engineering required.

steps.title: Review the outcome
steps.body: AM delivers the result — a PR, a resolved ticket, a groomed sprint, a stakeholder update. It explains what it did and why. You review, approve, and ship. It learns from your feedback.

comparisons.headline: AM vs the alternatives
comparisons.subhead: Single-function agents are too narrow. Generic assistants are too passive. AM is the unified digital worker for the whole team — and she works alongside whatever tools you already use.

items.body: Devin is engineering-only — it takes a ticket and writes code. AM does the same for developers, but also covers PM workflows and support. One worker, three team functions, at significantly lower cost per outcome.
items.title: AM vs Devin

items.body: Fin is excellent at support — but only on Intercom. AM is ecosystem-agnostic and works with Zendesk, Intercom, email, Slack, and custom ticketing. It also covers PM and developer workflows Fin cannot touch.
items.title: AM vs Intercom Fin

items.body: Linear AI and Notion AI are assistants — they help you work faster, but you are still doing the work. AM is a worker — it owns outcomes end-to-end. Tell AM to groom the sprint and it grooms the sprint.
items.title: AM vs Linear AI / Notion AI

items.body: Project management boards track work — they do not do it. AM keeps her own board but can be linked to Monday.com, Jira, Asana, or any system with an API. Your existing workflows stay intact; AM just starts doing the work inside them.
items.title: AM vs Monday.com / Jira / Asana

items.body: No single platform covers PM + developer + support under one worker with shared context. AM bridges all three — so when a customer ticket surfaces a product bug, the same worker that resolved the ticket can triage the bug and draft the fix. That cross-function compounding advantage grows over time.
items.title: The gap AM owns

plans.headline: We're here when you need us.
plans.subhead: Am runs on your device — no subscriptions, no monthly fees. Support is available free by email, or live with the founder when you need hands-on help.

free.title: Email Support
free.subtitle: Included with every Am. Always free.

live.title: Live Session
live.subtitle: Screenshare directly with Michael. Book as needed.
features: Book as many sessions as you need

plans.footer: No subscriptions. No recurring fees. Pay only when you want live help. Need something beyond that?

modal.successBody: We'll email you at {email} within 24 hours to schedule your Zoom session with Michael.
modal.successReceipt: Check your email for your receipt.

manifesto.p1: They just happen to make extraordinary personal assistants.
manifesto.p2: AM can plan your social, love, work, and home life — your health, your social media, your scheduling — all while writing software for you and your industry. She runs in the background, remembers everything, and gets sharper the longer she works with you.
manifesto.p3: Most AI companies are building tools. We are building a worker.
manifesto.p4: AM is not a copilot. Not a chatbot. Not a productivity feature bolted onto your stack. She is an autonomous teammate — one that picks up work, does it, and reports back. One that knows your codebase, your customers, and your team’s patterns. One that owns outcomes, not just responses.
manifesto.contextBody: Every other AI tool collects context and hopes it helps. AM engineers the right context for every step of every task — reading the right files, pulling the right history, surfacing the right decisions — before it acts. Not a bigger memory. A sharper mind.
manifesto.contextCloser: The result is work that is actually correct the first time — not a well-intentioned first draft that needs you to fix it.
manifesto.p5: Copilots respond to prompts. Assistants save you time. Workers are accountable for results. That is the difference between a tool and a teammate — and it is the only category we are building in.
manifesto.diffTitle: What makes a digital worker different

differentiators.label: Role-complete
differentiators.body: Does a whole job function, not just individual tasks

differentiators.label: Persistent context
differentiators.body: Learns the codebase, customers, and team patterns over time

differentiators.label: Outcome ownership
differentiators.body: Accountable for a result, not just a response

differentiators.label: Escalates, doesn't fail
differentiators.body: Knows when to hand off — and does it with full context

quotes.name: Sarah Chen

quotes.quote: We assigned AM our entire migration backlog — 200+ tickets. It opened PRs, wrote tests, and got 90% merged in three weeks. Our engineers finally have time to build.
quotes.name: Marcus Rivera

quotes.quote: Sprint grooming used to take half a day. AM does it overnight. We show up Monday morning with a fully groomed board and stakeholder updates already drafted.
quotes.name: Priya Nair

pilot.subhead: AM is free and open source. Every person on your team can run their own — a PM's AM, a dev's AM, a support AM. 5 people. The output of 20.
pilot.ctaNote: Open source and self-hosted. Deploy in minutes — no waitlist, no sales call.
